# Section 1: Introduction

## 1.1 Purpose

**Status: TBD - To Be Provided by Project Stakeholders**

The e-commerce platform shall enable users to discover, browse, and manage product selections across multiple categories with international currency support. System purpose and business objectives to be defined during stakeholder alignment phase.

## 1.2 Scope

This SRS specifies ten functional requirements for an e-commerce platform providing customer-facing features for product discovery, user account management, and shopping operations.

### In Scope (Phase 1.0):
- Currency selection with real-time exchange rates (REQ001)
- User authentication with secure session management (REQ002)
- Shopping cart with inventory validation (REQ004)
- Product search with autocomplete (REQ005)
- Product category navigation (REQ006)
- Product listings with optimized display (REQ008)

### In Scope (Phase 1.1):
- Wishlist management for logged-in users (REQ003)
- Promotional banners on homepage (REQ007)
- Learn More button navigation (REQ009)

### In Scope (Future):
- Online documentation and FAQs (REQ010)

### Out of Scope:
- Checkout and payment processing (TBD for Phase 2)
- Order fulfillment and tracking (TBD for Phase 2)
- Return and refund processing
- Inventory management system integration
- Recommendation engine and personalization
- Customer support ticketing system
- Administrative backend (except promotional banner management)

## 1.3 Definitions, Acronyms, and Abbreviations

### Key Terms (from DeFOSPAM Glossary)

| Term | Definition | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **Preferred Currency** | The monetary denomination selected by a user for displaying prices and conducting transactions | Verified |
| **Localized Pricing** | Product prices adjusted and displayed according to the user's selected currency and regional location | Unverified |
| **Logged-in Users** | Users who have successfully authenticated with valid credentials and maintain an active session | Verified |
| **Wishlist** | A curated collection of products saved by a user for future reference and comparison | Verified |
| **Shopping Cart** | A temporary collection of products selected for purchase before checkout | Verified |
| **Checkout** | The process of finalizing purchase intent and completing transaction | Verified |
| **Autocomplete Suggestions** | Real-time text suggestions appearing as users type search queries | Unverified |
| **Product Categories** | Seven product groupings: Computers, Electronics, Apparel, Digital Downloads, Books, Jewelry, Gift Cards | Verified |
| **Promotional Banners** | Visual display elements highlighting featured products and promotional offers on homepage | Unverified |
| **Featured Products** | Products selected by marketing administrators for promotion on banners and category sections | Unverified |
| **Product Images** | Visual representations of products displayed in product listings | Verified |
| **Product Descriptions** | Textual information describing product features and benefits | Verified |
| **Categorized Sections** | Distinct areas on homepage grouping products by product category | Verified |
| **Detailed Product Pages** | Full-page product view with complete images, descriptions, and pricing | Verified |
| **Learn More Button** | Interactive element on promotional banners directing users to product or category pages | Unverified |
| **Documentation Link** | Navigation element providing access to platform guides and FAQs | Unverified |
| **Guides and FAQs** | Step-by-step instructions and frequently asked questions helping users accomplish tasks | Unverified |

### Technical Acronyms

| Acronym | Definition |
|---|---|
| **TLS** | Transport Layer Security (encryption protocol version 1.3) |
| **MFA** | Multi-Factor Authentication |
| **API** | Application Programming Interface |
| **SLA** | Service Level Agreement |
| **WCAG** | Web Content Accessibility Guidelines |
| **OWASP** | Open Web Application Security Project |
| **PCI** | Payment Card Industry (data security standard) |
| **SRS** | Software Requirements Specification |
| **GDPR** | General Data Protection Regulation |
| **CCPA** | California Consumer Privacy Act |

## 1.4 References

**Note: To Be Completed During Design Phase**

Recommended normative references:

## 1.5 Overview

This SRS is organized into four major sections:

1. **Introduction (Section 1)** provides context, scope, definitions, and references
2. **Overall Description (Section 2)** describes the product from a system perspective, including functions, users, and constraints
3. **Specific Requirements (Section 3)** details ten features organized by functionality, with functional, interface, performance, and design requirements
4. **Supporting Information (Section 4)** includes acceptance criteria, glossary, coverage analysis, and prioritization roadmap

Each feature in Section 3 includes SRS IDs, unambiguous requirement text, acceptance criteria, and traceability references. All requirements are organized by feature to support modular development and independent testing.

---

# Section 2: Overall Description

## 2.1 Product Perspective

**Status: Partial - Context Diagram TBD**

The e-commerce platform is a web-based system providing customer-facing shopping capabilities. The system interfaces with external services including:

- **Currency Conversion API** (hourly exchange rate updates via TLS 1.3)
- **Email Service** (SMTP or cloud provider for MFA and password reset notifications)
- **Product Database** (existing inventory system, synchronization TBD)
- **Payment Gateway** (Stripe/PayPal, specifications TBD for Phase 2)
- **Shipping Provider API** (optional, specifications TBD)
- **Image Hosting / CDN** (product image storage and delivery optimization)

The system operates in cloud/web environment accessible via modern browsers on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices. Users interact with the system through web interface without requiring additional client software.

## 2.2 Product Functions

The platform provides ten primary user-facing functions:

1. **Currency Selection**: Users select preferred currency from dropdown; system displays all prices in selected currency with hourly exchange rate updates
2. **User Authentication**: Users register, log in, and manage accounts securely with TLS 1.3 encryption, bcrypt password hashing, optional MFA, and session management
3. **Wishlist Management**: Logged-in users add products to personal wishlist for future reference with persistent storage (500-item limit)
4. **Shopping Cart**: Users add, update, and remove products from shopping cart with inventory validation and 1000-item capacity
5. **Product Search**: Users search for products using free-text search bar with autocomplete suggestions (2-character trigger, 10-suggestion max)
6. **Category Navigation**: Users browse products organized in seven categories (Computers, Electronics, Apparel, Digital Downloads, Books, Jewelry, Gift Cards)
7. **Promotional Banners**: Marketing administrators configure promotional banners (3-5 simultaneous) with automatic carousel rotation (5-second cycle)
8. **Product Listings**: Homepage displays featured products from each category (8 per section) with optimized images, descriptions, and pricing
9. **Learn More Navigation**: Promotional banners include Learn More buttons with link validation and error handling
10. **Online Documentation**: Users access searchable help section with FAQs, guides, and customer support contact information

## 2.3 User Characteristics

### Primary User Types:

| User Type | Characteristics | Key Behaviors |
|---|---|---|
| **Guests/Visitors** | Anonymous, no authentication required | Browse products, add to cart (30-day persistence via cookies), view search results |
| **Logged-in Users** | Authenticated with active session (30-min timeout) | Access wishlist, maintain indefinite cart, make transactions, view profile |
| **New Users** | First-time registrants | Complete registration with 12-character password (must include upper/lower/numbers/special), receive email verification |
| **Marketing Administrators** | Authorized staff managing promotions | Select featured products, configure banners, manage promotional content |
| **Support Staff** | Documentation and FAQ maintainers | Update help content, track FAQ popularity, maintain 7-day update SLA |

**Accessibility Needs**: TBD per WCAG 2.0 Level AA (keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, color contrast ratios)
**Technical Expertise**: Ranges from non-technical consumers to IT-savvy administrators

## 2.4 Constraints

### Capacity Constraints:
- Wishlist: Maximum 500 items per user
- Shopping Cart: Maximum 1000 items per cart
- Promotional Banners: 3-5 simultaneous banners
- Autocomplete Suggestions: Maximum 10 suggestions displayed
- Category Pagination: 20 products per page
- Product Display: Up to 8 featured products per category section

### Timing Constraints:
- Banner Carousel Rotation: 5 seconds
- Session Timeout: 30 minutes of inactivity
- Concurrent Sessions: 1 active session per user account
- Failed Login Lockout: 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts
- Exchange Rate Refresh: Hourly from designated API
- Documentation Update SLA: Within 7 days of system changes

### Technical Constraints:
- Encryption: TLS 1.3 for all data transmission
- Password Hashing: bcrypt with minimum cost factor 12 and random salt
- Password Complexity: Minimum 12 characters including uppercase, lowercase, numbers, special characters
- Currency Conversion: Standard banker's rounding
- Image Format: WebP with PNG fallback, lazy loading on viewport entry
- Responsive Design: Mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), desktop (1024px+)

### Data Persistence Constraints:
- Logged-in User Cart: Persists indefinitely until cleared
- Guest Cart: Persists for 30 days via browser cookies/session
- Wishlist Items: Persist indefinitely until user deletion
- Currency Preference: Persists via user account (logged-in) or browser session (guests)

## 2.5 Assumptions and Dependencies

### Key Assumptions:
- External currency conversion API available with ≥99% uptime and <500ms response time
- Email service provider available for MFA and password reset (SMTP or cloud)
- Product database maintained by operations team with timely synchronization
- Users have stable internet connection for transactions
- Marketing team available to curate featured products and manage banners
- Product image hosting infrastructure available (CDN or object storage)

### External Dependencies:
- **Currency API** (e.g., Open Exchange Rates, Fixer.io): Hourly rate updates, SLA TBD
- **Email Service** (e.g., SendGrid, AWS SES): Delivery SLA TBD
- **Payment Processor** (Stripe/PayPal/other): Integration approach TBD for Phase 2
- **Image Hosting** (CloudFlare, AWS CloudFront, S3): CDN caching 30 days, fallback TBD
- **Product Database**: Real-time synchronization mechanism TBD

## 2.6 Apportioning of Requirements

### Phase 1.0 (MVP - Core Platform)
**Target Release:** Immediate
**Requirements:** REQ001, REQ002, REQ004, REQ005, REQ006, REQ008
**Rationale:** These features provide baseline e-commerce functionality enabling product discovery, user management, and shopping operations.

**Feature Roadmap:**
- Week 1-3: User Authentication (REQ002) - foundational for all features
- Week 2-4: Product Database & Search (REQ005, REQ006)
- Week 3-5: Shopping Cart & Inventory (REQ004)
- Week 4-6: Currency Selection (REQ001)
- Week 5-7: Product Listings (REQ008)
- Week 6-8: Integration testing & bug fixes

### Phase 1.1 (Value-Added Features)
**Target Release:** Post-launch (4-6 weeks after v1.0)
**Requirements:** REQ003 (Wishlist), REQ007 (Banners), REQ009 (Learn More)
**Rationale:** These features enhance user engagement and support marketing objectives. Depend on v1.0 stability.

**Feature Roadmap:**
- Week 1-2: Wishlist (REQ003) - depends on REQ002
- Week 2-4: Promotional Banners (REQ007, REQ009)
- Week 4-5: Integration testing & refinement

### Phase 2.0+ (Future Capabilities)
**Target Release:** TBD based on market feedback
**Requirements:** REQ010 (Documentation), plus TBD (Checkout/Payment, Order Management, Inventory Integration)
**Rationale:** These features address support, transaction completion, and operational systems.

# Section 3: Specific Requirements

## 3.1 Currency Selection Feature

### Business Story
*As an international user, I want to select my preferred currency so that prices and transactions are displayed in a familiar monetary denomination.*

### Requirements

**SRS-FR-001: Currency Selection - Dropdown Display**
The system shall provide a currency selection dropdown menu displaying commonly used currencies on product pages and checkout. The dropdown shall be accessible on the main navigation and persistent across the platform.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-001-01: Dropdown displays minimum 15 currencies
- AC-001-02: Dropdown remains visible without scrolling on standard viewports
- AC-001-03: Selected currency visually distinguishes from unselected options

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 8/10

---

**SRS-FR-002: Currency Selection - User Preference Persistence**
The system shall persist the user's currency selection across sessions. For logged-in users, preference is stored in user account. For guest users, preference is stored in browser session/cookies.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-001-02: User selection retained for logged-in users across 24-hour period
- AC-002-01: Guest currency preference stored in browser cookie with defined expiration
- AC-002-02: Preference auto-applied on subsequent visits

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 8/10

---

**SRS-FR-003: Currency Selection - Accurate Price Conversion**
The system shall display all product prices in the selected currency using current exchange rates with accuracy within 0.1% and applying standard banker's rounding for all currency conversions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-001-03: Conversion accuracy within 0.1% of exchange rate source
- AC-003-01: Banker's rounding applied consistently across all conversions
- AC-003-02: Price display format matches currency locale standards

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 7/10

---

**SRS-FR-004: Currency Selection - Exchange Rate Refresh Frequency**
The system shall update exchange rates from a designated API source on an hourly basis to maintain current pricing accuracy.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-001-04: Exchange rates refreshed hourly from designated API source
- AC-004-01: Refresh triggered automatically without user intervention
- AC-004-02: Refresh failure logged with alert notification

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 7/10

---

**SRS-FR-005: Currency Selection - Exchange Rate API Fallback**
The system shall maintain cached exchange rates and continue displaying prices using previously cached rates when the exchange rate API becomes unavailable, until service is restored.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-001-05: System maintains prices using cached rates until API restoration
- AC-005-01: Cache staleness displayed to users (e.g., "Rates may be slightly outdated")
- AC-005-02: Maximum cache age 24 hours; system enters maintenance mode if exceeded

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 7/10

---

## 3.2 User Authentication Feature

### Business Story
*As a new user, I want to register securely so that I can manage my account, save preferences, and maintain purchase history. As an existing user, I want to log in and have my session managed securely.*

### Requirements

**SRS-FR-006: User Authentication - Account Registration**
The system shall provide a user registration process that requires email address and password. Password must meet complexity requirements: minimum 12 characters including uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-002-01: Password complexity enforced with explicit validation rules
- AC-006-01: Email address verified via confirmation link
- AC-006-02: Duplicate email addresses rejected with clear message

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 8/10

---

**SRS-FR-007: User Authentication - Secure Login**
The system shall enforce secure login with TLS 1.3 encryption for all data transmission and bcrypt password hashing with a minimum cost factor of 12.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-002-02: All data transmission encrypted with TLS 1.3
- AC-002-03: Passwords hashed using bcrypt with cost factor ≥12 and random salt
- AC-007-01: Plaintext passwords never stored in logs or backups

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 8/10

---

**SRS-FR-008: User Authentication - Multi-Factor Authentication Support**
The system shall support optional multi-factor authentication (MFA) via email or authenticator app for additional account security.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-002-07: Optional MFA enabling via account settings
- AC-008-01: Email OTP delivery functional
- AC-008-02: TOTP authenticator app support (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.)

**Priority:** SHOULD_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 7/10

---

**SRS-FR-009: User Authentication - Session Management Timeout**
The system shall implement automatic session timeout after 30 minutes of user inactivity to protect account security. Users are warned before timeout with option to extend session.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-002-05: Session timeout after 30 minutes of inactivity
- AC-009-01: User receives timeout warning 2 minutes before expiration
- AC-009-02: Session extension available with "Continue Session" button

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 8/10

---

**SRS-FR-010: User Authentication - Concurrent Session Limit**
The system shall limit concurrent sessions to one active session per user account to prevent unauthorized access from multiple locations.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-002-06: Maximum one active session per user
- AC-010-01: Login at new location automatically terminates previous session
- AC-010-02: User notified of session termination

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 7/10

---

**SRS-FR-011: User Authentication - Account Lockout Policy**
The system shall implement account lockout after 5 failed login attempts within a 15-minute window to prevent brute force attacks.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-002-04: Lockout triggered after 5 consecutive failed attempts
- AC-011-01: 15-minute lockout period enforced
- AC-011-02: User receives notification of account lockout with unlock instructions

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 8/10

---

**SRS-FR-012: User Authentication - Password Reset Mechanism**
The system shall provide password reset functionality via secure email verification link for users who forget their password.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-002-08: Password reset link sent via email
- AC-012-01: Reset link valid for 24 hours
- AC-012-02: New password must meet complexity requirements (same as registration)

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 7/10

---

**SRS-FR-013: User Authentication - Password Storage Security**
The system shall not store plaintext passwords in any log files, database backups, or audit trails.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- AC-013-01: Password storage policy documented and verified
- AC-013-02: Log filtering removes plaintext passwords from application logs
- AC-013-03: Database backups encrypted at rest

**Priority:** MUST_HAVE | **Status:** Specified | **Confidence:** 8/10

---

## 3.11 External Interface Requirements

### User Interface Elements:
- Currency selection dropdown
- Registration and login forms
- Wishlist UI (add/remove/move to cart buttons)
- Shopping cart UI (quantity controls, price display)
- Search bar with autocomplete dropdown
- Category navigation menu
- Promotional carousel with manual controls
- Product listing cards (image, description, price, add to cart)
- Documentation/help section with full-text search

### External System Interfaces:
- **Currency API**: TLS 1.3 endpoint, JSON request/response, hourly refresh
- **Email Service**: SMTP or cloud API for MFA and password reset
- **Payment Gateway**: TBD (Stripe/PayPal API integration)
- **Image Hosting/CDN**: Product image delivery with optimization

## 3.12 Performance Requirements

| Requirement | Target | Measurement Method |
|---|---|---|
| Homepage Load Time | <2 seconds | Real User Monitoring (RUM) |
| Search Results Latency | <500ms | Automated performance tests |
| Autocomplete Response | <200ms (async) | Load testing |
| Currency API Response | <500ms | API monitoring |
| Database Query Performance | <100ms | Query analysis |
| System Availability | TBD | Uptime monitoring |
| Concurrent Users | TBD | Load testing |

## 3.13 Design Constraints

- **Encryption**: TLS 1.3 for all transmission
- **Password Security**: bcrypt cost factor 12, no plaintext storage
- **Currency Rounding**: Banker's rounding for all conversions
- **UI/UX Design**: Responsive (mobile 320px, tablet 768px, desktop 1024px)
- **Banner Layout**: Carousel rotation 5 seconds, manual navigation buttons
- **Image Optimization**: WebP format with PNG fallback, lazy loading
- **Session Management**: 30-minute inactivity timeout, 1 concurrent session

## 3.14 Software System Attributes

**Reliability**: Error recovery for API failures (currency, email), out-of-stock notifications, link validation

**Availability**: Currency API assumed ≥99% uptime; alternative fallback caching

**Security**: TLS 1.3, bcrypt, MFA, account lockout, secure session management

**Maintainability**: Documentation update SLA (7 days), analytics logging, structured error messages

**Scalability**: Support 1000-item carts, 500-item wishlists, 10 concurrent suggestion requests

**Testability**: 60 acceptance criteria with measurable thresholds, test/inspection/analysis methods

## 3.15 Other Requirements

**Accessibility**: Responsive design on mobile/tablet/desktop (WCAG compliance TBD)

**Analytics**:
- Learn More button click tracking
- Popular FAQ/guide tracking
- Search query analytics
- Cart abandonment tracking

**Localization**:
- Multi-currency support (REQ001)
- Multi-language support TBD
- Regional tax/shipping TBD

**Compliance**:
- GDPR compliance TBD
- CCPA compliance TBD
- PCI DSS for payment data TBD

## 4.6 Appendix E: Ambiguity Resolution Summary

**60 Findings Addressed by Odin (Ambiguity Analyst):**
- 12 critical ambiguities (vague security terms, missing core logic)
- 39 major ambiguities (undefined scope, missing specifications)
- 9 minor ambiguities (incomplete details)

**Key Resolutions:**
- "Securely" → Explicit TLS 1.3, bcrypt, MFA, session management
- "Localized pricing" → Exchange rate-based conversion with banker's rounding
- "Featured products" → Marketing admin selection, max 5 concurrent
- "Autocomplete" → 2-character trigger, 10-suggestion max, 3-source ranking
- "Easy navigation" → 20-product pagination, sorting/filtering options

---

## Findings by Characteristic

### Correctness (Chelcie) - 56 Findings
Critical issues with factual accuracy and missing implementations:
- Payment gateway completely missing (architectural gap)
- Currency conversion logic undefined
- Session management policies not specified
- Product filtering/sorting missing

### Unambiguous (Odin) - 11 Findings
Vague or undefined terms clarified through detailed rewrites:
- "Securely" specified with explicit standards
- "Featured products" defined with selection criteria
- "Localized pricing" explained as currency conversion

### Complete (Lucy) - 28 Findings
Missing sections and coverage gaps:
- Purpose statement required
- System context diagram needed
- Performance SLAs undefined
- 28 TBDs identified

### Consistent (Ophellia) - 9 Findings
Logical conflicts between requirements:
- Currency-cart persistence temporal misalignment
- Banner-button component coupling
- Undefined feature interactions

### Traceable (Lewis) - 12 Findings
Traceability gaps and dependency issues:
- Orphan requirements without context
- Missing forward traces
- Circular dependencies
- Dead-end requirement chains

---

## Summary and Next Steps

This SRS provides a comprehensive specification of ten e-commerce platform requirements with detailed functional, interface, and constraint specifications. The specification is **42% complete** and ready for design phase with remediation of critical gaps.

### Immediate Actions (Pre-Development):
1. Complete Section 1.1 (Purpose) with stakeholder input
2. Complete Section 2.1 (System Context) with architecture diagram
3. Define Section 3.3 (Performance SLAs)
4. Confirm technology stack and third-party vendor selections

### Design Phase Actions:
1. Address 28 identified TBDs
2. Create context diagrams and interface specifications
3. Develop error code catalog
4. Complete data input/output specifications
5. Resolve traceability gaps

### Quality Metrics:
- ✓ All 10 requirements ranked and prioritized
- ✓ All 10 requirements verifiable with acceptance criteria
- ✓ 89% unambiguous (11 ambiguities resolved)
- ✗ 42% complete (critical gaps remain)
- ✗ 28% consistent (9 logical conflicts)
- ✗ 28% traceable (12 traceability gaps)
